mechanism_narrative: Positive trend in nonfarm payrolls signals ongoing economic expansion and rising household income, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not positive in greater than 55% of observations during 3-month rising PAYEMS trends over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, sustained restrictive monetary policy elevates discount rates and compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and retail sales reflects broad demand momentum that translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; the joint signal is stronger than either series in isolation.
failure_condition: The joint signal of INDPRO and RSAFS does not outperform the univariate PAYEMS signal by at least 2 percentage points in directional accuracy over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1%, elevated CPI inflation reduces real corporate cash flows and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 1%, a 1 standard deviation increase in CPIAUCSL growth does not correspond to negative 20-day forward returns in at least 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production convey positive supply-side earnings surprises that predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not positive following month-over-month INDPRO increases in more than 52% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative rise in the unemployment rate greater than 0.4 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that drives negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.4pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in housing starts and retail sales above their respective thresholds confirm a consumer-led investment expansion that predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases > 5% month-over-month and RSAFS increases > 2% month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint trigger of HOUST and RSAFS produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth positively surprises relative to recent trend, leading to upward revisions in aggregate demand expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive quarterly GDPC1 growth does not correspond to positive 20-day forward returns in at least 60% of quarters, or the Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 20-day returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year window.
